mission.title: redpanda: config: output cluster configuration schema as a build step
mission.description: config: output cluster configuration schema as a build step. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current redpanda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your redpanda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
helm list -n redpanda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working redpanda installation.

steps.title: Review redpanda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant redpanda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get configmap -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=redpanda
```
This is for consumption by cloud team (and maybe docs team later), when they would like to know the config schema for a given redpanda version without having to query a running redpanda instance.

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for config: output cluster configuration schema as a build step
steps.description:
## Cover letter

Adds a general utility binary that includes all developer facing utilities that core
intends to expose and are not included in the redpanda service binary.

```
bin/rp_util --help
Allowed options:
  --help                Allowed options
  --config_schema_json  Generates JSON schema
```yaml
bin/rp_util --help
Allowed options:
  --help                Allowed options
  --config_schema_json  Generates JSON schema for cluster configuration
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get events -n redpanda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "config: output cluster configuration schema as a build step" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Cover letter

Adds a general utility binary that includes all developer facing utilities that core
intends to expose and are not included in the redpanda service binary.

```
bin/rp_util --help
Allowed options:
  --help                Allowed options
  --config_schema_json  Generates JSON schema for cluster configuration
```

## Release notes
* none

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with redpanda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
